description: SRE Agent — Kubernetes Cluster Health. Current issues with root-cause + fix recommendations, historical pod lifecycle, per-pod cost, and node pressure.

panels.title: 🔥 Active Issues — with Root Cause & Fix Recommendations

panels.description: Active check types with severity and count. Filters out zero-count entries in Grafana (not in PromQL) to avoid race-condition blanks during scrape.
panels.title: Active Issues — Root Cause & Recommended Actions
📋 Recommendations Reference — all check types
panels.description: Static reference. Always available regardless of which image version is running.

options.content:
📋 Recommendations Reference — all 23 check types

This panel is always available. The "Active Issues" table above shows only currently firing checks.

Check Type 🔍 Root Cause ⚡ Immediate Action 🛡 Prevent
NodeNotReady Node process (kubelet/CRI-O) crashed or lost connectivity to control plane. Can also be disk/memory exhaustion. systemctl status kubelet; journalctl -u kubelet -n 50; systemctl restart kubelet if needed. Enable OKE node auto-repair. Deploy Node Problem Detector DaemonSet.
NodeDiskPressure Free disk dropped below eviction threshold (~1 GiB). Caused by image layer accumulation (imagePullPolicy:Always + old 0/0/0 RSes) or containers with no ephemeral-storage limit. On node: crictl rmi --prune. Delete old 0/0/0 RSes. Delete evicted pods: kubectl delete pods -A --field-selector=status.phase=Failed Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent. Set revisionHistoryLimit: 2. Add resources.limits.ephemeral-storage to every container.
NodeMemoryPressure Node memory is exhausted. Containers with no memory limits can expand unbounded. kubectl top pods --all-namespaces --sort-by=memory — identify and restart the biggest consumer. Set memory limits on all containers. Use VPA to auto-tune. Distribute workloads evenly.
PodEvicted Pod forcibly terminated by kubelet due to DiskPressure (ephemeral-storage) or MemoryPressure. The pod.status.message contains the exact reason. kubectl delete pod <pod> -n <ns> to remove artifact. kubectl describe node <node> for pressure status. crictl rmi --prune if DiskPressure. Add ephemeral-storage limits. Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent. Set revisionHistoryLimit: 2.
OOMKilled Container exceeded memory limit and was killed by Linux OOM killer. kubectl top pod <pod> -n <ns> to see usage. kubectl logs <pod> --previous to see crash. Increase memory limit temporarily. Raise limit. Use VPA to auto-tune. JVM: set -Xmx below the container limit. Profile for memory leaks.
CrashLoopBackOff Container starts, crashes, restarts in a loop. Bad config, missing env/secret, dependency not ready, or app crash. kubectl logs <pod> -n <ns> --previous. Exit 137=OOM, Exit 1=app error, Exit 143=probe killed it. Add init containers to wait for dependencies. Use readinessProbe. Validate env vars at startup.
ImagePullBackOff Cannot pull image: tag missing, Docker Hub rate limit (100 pulls/6h), expired imagePullSecret, or registry unreachable. kubectl describe pod <pod> for exact error. Verify imagePullSecret: kubectl get secret ocir-secret -n <ns>. Push all images to private OCIR. Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent. Use image digests, not mutable tags.
PodPendingTooLong Scheduler cannot place the pod: insufficient resources, nodeSelector/affinity mismatch, PVC not bound, or taint mismatch. kubectl describe pod <pod> → Events → FailedScheduling for reason. kubectl top nodes for resources. Set accurate resource requests. Use Cluster Autoscaler. Audit nodeSelector labels.
HighRestartCount Container restarting repeatedly: OOMKills, app crashes, or aggressive liveness probe. kubectl logs <pod> --previous. Exit 137=OOM (raise limit). Exit 1=app error. Exit 143=tune probe. Fix root crash. Tune liveness probe (failureThreshold, initialDelaySeconds). Use VPA for memory.
DeadPodNotCleaned Failed pod sitting for too long. Terminated pods still consume ephemeral-storage (log files) on the node. kubectl delete pod <pod> -n <ns>. Bulk: kubectl delete pods -A --field-selector=status.phase=Failed Use ttlSecondsAfterFinished on Jobs. Kubernetes 1.23+ auto-GCs via --terminated-pod-gc-threshold.
PVCLost PersistentVolume backing this PVC was deleted or is inaccessible. Pods using it will fail to start. kubectl describe pvc <name> -n <ns>. Check block volume in OCI console. Restore from backup. Never delete PVs manually. Use StorageClass reclaimPolicy: Retain. Backup with Velero or OCI Block Volume snapshots.
PVCPending PVC waiting for PV: StorageClass not configured, volume quota exceeded, or provisioner not running. kubectl describe pvc <name> → Events for provisioner errors. kubectl get storageclass. Pre-provision volumes for stateful workloads. Monitor storage quota. Use WaitForFirstConsumer binding mode.
ServiceNoEndpoints Service selector doesn't match any running pod labels, or all pods fail readinessProbe. kubectl get pods -n <ns> -l <selector>. kubectl describe endpoints <svc> -n <ns>. Use consistent label conventions. Test selectors before applying. Add readinessProbes.
OldReplicaSets Old 0/0/0 ReplicaSets holding image layer references on disk, preventing container runtime GC. kubectl get rs -n <ns> | awk 'NR>1&&$2==0&&$3==0{print $1}' | xargs kubectl delete rs -n <ns>. Then: crictl rmi --prune Set revisionHistoryLimit: 2. Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent.
ImagePullPolicyAlways Forces registry check on every pod start even if image is cached. Causes extra network traffic, slow starts, and layer accumulation. Change to IfNotPresent in the deployment spec. Safe when using unique immutable tags per build. Always use content-addressable tags (git SHA/semver). Never use :latest in production.
NoEphemeralStorageLimit Container with no ephemeral-storage limit can fill the entire node disk, evicting ALL pods on that node. Add resources.limits.ephemeral-storage: 1Gi to the container spec. Enforce via LimitRange. Configure app log rotation. Use log-shipping sidecar (Promtail/Fluentd).
MemLimitOvercommit Total memory limits > 120% of node allocatable. If all pods hit limits simultaneously, OOM killer will cascade. kubectl top pods --all-namespaces --sort-by=memory. Move workloads to other nodes via affinity rules. Set limits ≤ 2× requests. Use VPA. Add more nodes or use larger shapes.
CPULimitOvercommit Total CPU limits exceed node allocatable. Causes throttling and degraded response times. kubectl top pods --all-namespaces --sort-by=cpu to find top consumers. Set CPU limits = requests for predictable performance. Spread workloads across more nodes.
JobFailed Job has failed attempts. May still be retrying up to backoffLimit. kubectl logs -l job-name=<name> -n <ns> --tail=100. kubectl describe job <name> for backoff status. Add error handling + retry logic in job code. Set backoffLimit and activeDeadlineSeconds.
DaemonSetNotReady Node missing a ready DaemonSet pod: taint without toleration, insufficient resources, or pod crash. kubectl rollout status ds/<name> -n <ns>. kubectl get pods -n <ns> -l <selector> -o wide. Ensure tolerations cover all node taints. Set appropriate resource requests.
StatefulSetNotReady StatefulSet pods not ready. Upgrades are sequential — one failing pod blocks the entire rollout. kubectl get pods -n <ns> -l <selector>. kubectl describe pod to see Events. Ensure storage is provisioned. Use proper readinessProbe. Test startup sequence on staging.
NoPodRequests Pods without resource requests are BestEffort QoS — evicted FIRST under pressure. Add resource requests (even cpu: 10m, memory: 64Mi) to make pod Burstable. Enforce via LimitRange with defaults. Use VPA in recommendation mode.
HighRevisionHistory High revisionHistoryLimit keeps many old RSes around, each pinning image layers on node disk. kubectl patch deploy <name> -n <ns> --type=json -p '[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/revisionHistoryLimit","value":2}]' Set revisionHistoryLimit: 2 in all deployment templates. Enforce via OPA/Kyverno policy.

panels.title: New Evictions / OOMKills / CrashLoops — Cumulative Counter
panels.description: Counters only increment when a NEW issue appears for the first time. Persistent issues from prior cycles are not re-counted.

panels.title: 💰 Resource Consumption & Cost by Pod — Historical

panels.description: Actual CPU usage (not requests). Uses cAdvisor metrics scraped by kube-prometheus-stack. Shows top 10 pods by real consumption — useful for spotting runaway processes and cost attribution.
panels.title: Top 10 Pods — CPU Usage (actual cores)

panels.description: Actual memory working set. Top 10 pods by real memory consumption — use this to find memory leaks and right-size your resource limits.
panels.title: Top 10 Pods — Memory Usage (actual bytes)

panels.description: CPU usage (actual cores) grouped by namespace over time. Use the time range selector to see last week or last month.
panels.title: CPU Usage by Namespace (actual) — Historical

panels.description: Memory usage (actual bytes) grouped by namespace over time. Use the time range selector to see last week or last month.
panels.title: Memory Usage by Namespace (actual) — Historical

panels.description: Estimated monthly cost per namespace based on CPU + memory REQUESTS. Formula: (cpu_cores × $0.025/hr + mem_GiB × $0.0015/hr) × 720 hours/month. Adjust multipliers to match your OCI node shape pricing.
panels.title: Resource Requests & Estimated Monthly Cost by Namespace (snapshot)

panels.description: How much total memory limits exceed node allocatable memory. >100% means OOM kills are possible if all pods hit their limits simultaneously.
panels.title: Memory Limit Overcommit % per Node

panels.description: How much total CPU limits exceed node allocatable CPU. >100% means CPU throttling under load.
panels.title: CPU Limit Overcommit % per Node

panels.description: Number of Running pods per node over time. An uneven distribution can indicate a scheduling or resource problem.
panels.title: Pods per Node — Running

panels.description: Deployments that currently have fewer available replicas than desired. These are actively degraded workloads.
panels.title: Deployments with Unavailable Replicas

title: SRE Agent — Cluster Health
